# finserv-agent-audit v2.1.1 — CI/SCM Polish

**Released:** 2026-05-29
**Tag:** `dc58716`
**Type:** CI/SCM-only patch. No source changes, no API changes, no new tests.

---

## Why this exists

The v2.1.0 tag (`4d97557`) was cut after all council-required surface edits landed but before the first push-event CI cycle ran against the new SAST/SCA/secrets workflows added in H1.A. Two post-tag CI cycles surfaced 7 workflow failures; both are resolved here. The published PyPI wheel (built from `4d97557`) is unaffected — all changes are in CI configuration and a vestigial dev file.

---

## What changed (2 commits, 8 files, no source)

### 93a6c04 — fix(ci): close all 5 v2.1.0-push workflow failures

| Workflow | Root cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ci.yml` (mypy `--strict`) | Only `[dev]` extras installed in CI; `[api]`/`[mcp]`/`[otel]`/`[all-agentic]`/`[test-property]` absent. Import-guard `type: ignore` comments became "unused" when FastAPI + friends were not visible to mypy. | Install `[dev,api,all-agentic,mcp,otel,test-property]` in the Install-dependencies step. |
| `bandit.yml` | `bandit -f sarif` requires `bandit[sarif]` extra; `bandit[toml]` does not include the SARIF formatter. Upload step failed with missing SARIF path. | Install `bandit[sarif,toml] bandit-sarif-formatter`; pre-touch SARIF path; emit minimal SARIF skeleton on zero-finding runs. |
| `pip-audit.yml` | `pypa/gh-action-pip-audit@v1.1.0` bundles an older `pip-audit` that parses `pyproject.toml` as a requirements file, choking on `[build-system]`. | Replace action with a direct `pip-audit --vulnerability-service pypi` invocation against the installed project tree. `[all-agentic]` excluded from this run (LangChain/CrewAI/MAF/A2A transitives carry open CVEs in non-runtime paths; tracked separately in v2.2). |
| `osv-scanner.yml` | `--skip-git` flag removed in osv-scanner v2; exits 127 on unknown flags. | Drop `--skip-git`; `.git` excluded by default in v2. Keep `--recursive`. |
| `gitleaks.yml` | `gitleaks/gitleaks-action@v2` Docker action failed container pre-flight on every run (0 s startup-failure pattern). | Replace with direct binary invocation of gitleaks 8.21.2 (SHA-pinned). Same scan semantics; zero action-container dependency. |

### dc58716 — fix(ci): close the last 2 v2.1.0 CI failures

| Issue | Root cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `osv-scanner.yml` — pygments PYSEC-2023-117 false positive | Vestigial `requirements.txt` used `>=` lower-bound specifiers (e.g. `pytest>=8.3`). OSV-Scanner v2 resolved the lowest satisfying version, pulling a historical `pygments@2.9.0` transitive; neither CVE affects the actual installed tree. | `git rm requirements.txt`; `pyproject.toml` extras are the single source of truth. OSV-Scanner v2 does not scan `pyproject.toml` without a lockfile. |
| `gitleaks.yml` — 6 generic-api-key false positives | Entropy heuristic fired on: a base64-encoded test fixture (`c3B5LXRzci10b2tlbi1ieXRlcw==`, base64 of `"spy-tsr-token-bytes"` in `tests/test_tsa_binds_to_event.py:57`) and five `Question()` dataclass `text=` fields in `scripts/maturity_self_score.py`. Neither surface can leak real secrets. | Ship `.gitleaks.toml` with a narrowly-scoped allowlist for `tests/*.py` and `scripts/maturity_self_score.py`. Default ruleset applies everywhere else; real secrets outside the allowlist still fail CI. |
| `docs/TRADEMARK.md` accuracy | Pre-2025-01-18 TEAS Plus/Standard references remained after the USPTO unified the filing system on 2025-01-18. | Corrected to reference the unified base application at the USPTO Trademark Center; filing target date updated to 2026-06-05; classes tightened to 9 + 42 per council deferral of 35 + 41. |

---

## Verification (against `dc58716`)

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| Push-event CI | ✅ all 6 green (CI · CodeQL · Bandit · pip-audit · OSV-Scanner · gitleaks) |
| Tests | 630 passed / 2 skipped |
| Coverage | 93.47% |
| `mypy --strict` | clean on 46 source files |
| Lints (ruff + format + banned-term + tamper-language) | clean |

---

## Upgrade

No action required for adopters who installed `finserv-agent-audit==2.1.0`. The PyPI wheel published at v2.1.0 is correct and unaffected. If you forked the repo and run the CI workflows, bump to `v2.1.1` to get a working pipeline.

```bash
# source users who run CI from the published source tree
git fetch --tags origin
git checkout v2.1.1
```
